Home › Seminole County › Altamonte Springs › FIG'S PRIME
F 59

FIG'S PRIME

This grade is calculated by us from Florida DBPR public inspection records — Florida does not issue official grades. How we calculate it →

Data last updated: June 17, 2026

Loved (4.5★) but failing (F, 59/100) · safer than 3% of Seminole County

The crowd and the inspector disagree about FIG'S PRIME, and it isn't close: 984 reviewers settle on 4.5 stars, while its latest health inspection lands at an F, 59/100.

That F comes from 2 high-priority, 5 intermediate, and 10 basic violations cited on June 17, 2026. It currently ranks safer than 3% of Seminole County restaurants we grade — close to the bottom of the county. And the direction isn't reassuring: its recent inspections have been sliding, not recovering. If you want the flavor with fewer question marks, the safer-options list below maps higher-graded kitchens within a short drive.

Get directions Call (407) 960-1300

Safer options nearby

Higher-graded restaurants close to FIG'S PRIME, by Florida DBPR grade:

  • A
    TERRA MIA BRICK OVEN

    4.5★ 0.1 mi away

  • A
    MELTING POT

    4.6★ 0.1 mi away

  • A
    KOBE JAPANESE STEAKHOUSE

    4.8★ 0.2 mi away

Inspection history

The following records are from Florida DBPR public inspection records, most recent first.

  1. June 17, 2026 · Routine - Food

    Inspection Completed - No Further Action

    2 high-priority · 5 intermediate · 10 basic

    • 02 — Consumer advisory, date-marking & labeling of ready-to-eat foods (×2)
    • 03 — Temperature control of foods (cold/hot holding, cooking, cooling, reheating)
    • 08 — Cross-contamination: raw foods separated; food protected during prep/storage
    • 10 — In-use food dispensing utensils properly stored (×2)
    • 12 — Handwashing; employee eating/drinking/smoking practices
    • 16 — Dishwashing facilities; sanitizer test kits; gauges
    • 22 — Food-contact surfaces clean and sanitized (×2)
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 29 — Plumbing installed & maintained; mop sink; backflow prevention
    • 31 — Handwash sinks accessible; handwashing supplies & sign provided
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation
    • 40 — Employee personal belongings
    • 50 — Current license, properly displayed
    • 52 — Misrepresentation / misbranding

  2. March 25, 2026 · Routine - Food

    Call Back - Complied

    0 high-priority · 1 intermediate · 1 basic

    • 16 — Dishwashing facilities; sanitizer test kits; gauges
    • 21 — Wiping cloths; clean & soiled linens; laundry

  3. February 20, 2026 · Routine - Food

    Call Back - Extension given, pending

    0 high-priority · 2 intermediate · 1 basic

    • 16 — Dishwashing facilities; sanitizer test kits; gauges
    • 21 — Wiping cloths; clean & soiled linens; laundry
    • 53 — Food manager certification; employee food-safety training

  4. December 19, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Warning Issued

    1 high-priority · 2 intermediate · 2 basic

    • 09 — Bare-hand contact with ready-to-eat food
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained
    • 16 — Dishwashing facilities; sanitizer test kits; gauges
    • 21 — Wiping cloths; clean & soiled linens; laundry
    • 53 — Food manager certification; employee food-safety training
